top_signals.title: Czech Price Pressures Warrant Increased Caution for Central Bank
top_signals.summary: The Czech central bank said monetary policy caution remains warranted, with the headline inflation rate likely to return to or “slightly above” the 2% target this year following a recent drop.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Oil Steadies as US, Iran Continue Talks After Renewed Fighting
top_signals.summary: Oil steadied as the US and Iran continued talks despite a flare-up in fighting that strained their ceasefire and deterred tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Federal Reserve announces the leadership and objectives of its task forces to advance the conduct of monetary policy
top_signals.summary: Federal Reserve announces the leadership and objectives of its task forces to advance the conduct of monetary policy
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Philip R. Lane: AI and monetary policy
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services, May 2026
top_signals.summary: The U.S. goods and services trade deficit increased in May 2026 according to the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis and the U.S. Census Bureau. The deficit increased from $54.6 billion in April (revised) to $77.6 billion in May, as exports decreased and imports increased. The goods deficit increased $23.6 billion in May to $106.5 billion. The services surplus increased $0.6 billion in May to $28.9 billion. Full Text
